publicSideBand: The city replaces service lines during eligible water main projects or other city construction triggers
privateSideBand: Homeowners own the water service line to the meter and should confirm replacement cost with local contractors when work is not part of a city project
fullReplacementBand: No official local bid range is published and owner cost depends on whether the work is tied to a city main project or a private replacement
housingTypeAssumption: Ames publishes responsibility rules and project triggers rather than contractor bid ranges
permitAssumption: Eligibility for city-managed replacement depends on the current water main project and service line verification
restorationAssumption: Restoration and final scope vary by site and by whether the work is private or coordinated with a city project
methodologySummary: Ames does not publish a local replacement price and instead explains responsibility rules: the homeowner owns the line to the meter unless the replacement is folded into a city water-main or related construction project.
ownerTriggerSummary: Owners avoid direct cost only when Ames coordinates the line inside a current city project and otherwise stay on a private contractor path with site-specific pricing and restoration.
